role: Quran scholar; only woman elected to the 1979 Assembly of Experts for the Constitution
bio: Monireh Gorji was an Islamic scholar and the only woman among the 73 members of the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979. Elected from Tehran Province on the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party) ticket with over 1.3 million votes, she was a Quran scholar and mujtahid. Despite her IRP affiliation, she raised objections during the constitutional debates about restrictions being imposed on women in the name of Islam. She continued her scholarly career in Quranic studies and Islamic jurisprudence.
editorial_notes: Born 1929, died 2025. The sole woman in the 73-member constitutional assembly. Elected on IRP ticket from Tehran with 1,313,731 votes.
